articles.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed over Israeli strikes in Lebanon; US disputes claim
articles.summary: Iran's armed forces announced Saturday they would close the Strait of Hormuz — days after it reopened — alleging Washington failed to rein in Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon, in breach of the new ceasefire. US Central Command countered that commercial traffic actually rose and that 'Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz', saying US forces are monitoring to keep it flowing.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill 32 in Lebanon despite renewed Hezbollah ceasefire, putting US-Iran talks in peril
articles.summary: Israeli air raids and drone attacks in southern Lebanon continued after a renewed ceasefire with Hezbollah took effect Friday, killing at least 32 people. Hezbollah said a truce was 'meaningless' while Israeli forces remain on Lebanese soil and strikes continue; Israel accused the group of breaking the terms first. The fighting threatens to derail US-Iran follow-up talks.

articles.title: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah killed in Israeli strike on Gaza's Bureij camp
articles.summary: Ahmed Wishah, a cameraman for Al Jazeera Mubasher, was killed when an Israeli drone struck a home in the Bureij refugee camp; two others died. The IDF said he was a Hamas military-wing sniper operative but offered no immediate evidence; Al Jazeera called the accusation baseless. His correspondent brother was killed in an April strike. He is at least the 12th Al Jazeera media worker killed in Gaza since October 2023.

articles.title: Heading to Iran talks, Vance says he hopes for progress on Lebanon ceasefire and nuclear issue
articles.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance departed for Switzerland to join technical-level US-Iran talks in Burgenstock on Sunday, saying his priorities are structuring the negotiations, making progress on the nuclear file and securing a Lebanon ceasefire. Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi and parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf are attending; IAEA chief Rafael Grossi is expected to lead part of the nuclear discussions.

articles.title: Hezbollah says it confronted Israeli 'infiltration attempt' in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah said its fighters confronted an Israeli forces 'infiltration attempt' in the south, adding to a day of cross-border incidents as both sides accused each other of violating the renewed ceasefire.

articles.title: Saudi Navy floats first Sarawat Phase II warship at Spanish shipyard
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia floated its first Sarawat Phase II warship at a Spanish shipyard, marking progress in the kingdom's naval modernisation programme.

articles.title: Saudi relief agency trains over 1,000 vulnerable Gazans in vocational and digital skills
articles.summary: A Saudi relief agency said it had trained more than 1,000 vulnerable Gazans in vocational and digital skills, part of the kingdom's humanitarian outreach as the war's effects persist.

articles.title: New Israeli settler outpost established near Jurish, south of Nablus in occupied West Bank
articles.summary: Palestinians documented a newly-established Israeli settler outpost near the village of Jurish, south of Nablus, amid continued settlement expansion and displacement across the occupied West Bank that rights groups have described as forced displacement.

articles.title: Israeli strike on Gaza City apartment kills four as attacks continue across the strip
articles.summary: Four people were killed in an Israeli strike on an apartment in Gaza City, with further deaths reported in Khan Younis and central Gaza, Palestinian media said. The IDF did not immediately comment on the Gaza City strike. The strikes were part of a day that killed at least 11 across the territory.
